objective: Create a governance-owned graphify hook helper and installer so governed repos route graph refreshes through hldpro-governance manifest-defined output paths instead of raw graphify hooks that write into product checkouts.
scope_boundary: This plan covers governance issue #248 and child microslices #249, #250, and #251.
The implementation may add governance-owned helper scripts under scripts/knowledge_base, focused tests, documentation, closeout artifacts, and tracker updates.
The helper must resolve graph targets through docs/graphify_targets.json and must preflight output paths before invoking scripts/knowledge_base/build_graph.py.
Product repo adoption happens in separate AIS and knocktracker issues and PRs after the governance helper exists.
out_of_scope: Editing AIS or knocktracker source files from this governance plan.
Salvaging or approving the contaminated AIS remediation branch tracked in AIS #1101.
Changing upstream graphify behavior or graphify extraction semantics.
Treating local untracked .git/hooks containment as durable completion evidence.
Adding graph retrieval ranking, Neo4j, fine-tuning, or graph quality changes.
research_summary: AIS #1099 identified that raw graphify-installed hooks can call graphify.watch._rebuild_code(Path('.')), which writes graphify-out into the current product checkout. Governance already has the durable graph target manifest at docs/graphify_targets.json and the canonical builder at scripts/knowledge_base/build_graph.py. Issue #248 therefore needs a governance-owned installer/helper that resolves the governance root, governed repo slug, manifest paths, and hook targets before any build runs. Claude alternate review approved this approach with changes: add #248 to OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md before implementation, treat the cross-review as architecture scope, resolve manifest paths relative to governance root, preflight output paths before build_graph.py, pin MS2 handoff to gpt-5.3-codex-spark high, require Stage 6 closeout, and require AIS/knocktracker adoption to prove raw graphify hooks are removed or absent.

sprints.name: MS1 - Planning and Control Artifacts
sprints.goal: Make #248 issue-backed, reviewed, and ready for implementation without hidden local work.
tasks: Mirror #248 in OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md before helper implementation begins.
Create the #248 structured plan JSON and PDCAR companion.
Record alternate-model review status and required changes.
Define child microslices #249, #250, and #251 with acceptance criteria.
Defer the architecture cross-review artifact until a real PR number exists.
acceptance_criteria: OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md includes an In Progress row for #248.
docs/plans/issue-248-graphify-hook-helper-pdcar.md exists and matches the JSON plan.
alternate_model_review.status is accepted_with_followup and cites the Claude review.
execution_handoff names gpt-5.3-codex-spark with model_reasoning_effort=high for MS2.
Cross-review is documented as architecture scope and must use a real PR number before commit.

sprints.name: MS2 - Managed Runner and Installer
sprints.goal: Implement the governance-owned helper that installs managed hooks and routes graph refreshes through manifest-defined governance output paths.
tasks: Add a helper script under scripts/knowledge_base with resolve, dry-run, refresh, and install behavior.
Resolve governance root and repo slug safely from explicit flags or target repo root.
Resolve source_path, output_path, and wiki_path relative to the governance root and docs/graphify_targets.json.
Preflight output paths before calling build_graph.py so product repo graphify-out writes are refused.
Install managed post-commit and post-checkout hooks while refusing unmanaged hook overwrite unless explicit backup or force mode is selected.
Add focused tests for resolution, preflight refusal, existing-hook handling, and generated hook body content.
acceptance_criteria: Dry-run output includes repo slug, governance root, source path, output path, wiki path, and hook targets.
Manifest paths resolve relative to governance root rather than product repo CWD.
AIS and knocktracker can be inferred or passed explicitly by repo slug.
Unsafe product-repo output paths abort before build_graph.py is called.
Generated managed hooks do not contain graphify.watch._rebuild_code(Path('.')) or raw graphify hook install behavior.

sprints.name: MS3 - Docs, Adoption Handoff, and Closeout
sprints.goal: Document the helper, record adoption requirements, and complete Stage 6 closeout for #248.
tasks: Document install, dry-run, refresh, and adoption commands.
State that raw graphify hook install output is not the governed AIS/knocktracker path.
Update #248 with validation evidence and child issue status.
Record AIS and knocktracker adoption requirements: raw graphify hooks removed or absent, and helper dry-run evidence captured.
Create and validate the Stage 6 closeout artifact.
acceptance_criteria: Docs show helper commands for governed repos.
AIS and knocktracker adoption handoffs require raw hook removal or documented absence plus dry-run evidence.
#248 has validation evidence and adoption links.
hooks/closeout-hook.sh exits 0 for the closeout artifact.
Newly discovered required work is completed in-scope or captured as issue-backed follow-up before #248 closes.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm issue-backed structured plan, PDCAR, alternate review, and closeout workflow before implementation.
specialist_reviews.summary: The active workflow requires GitHub issue backing, a canonical structured JSON plan, PDCAR companion context, accepted alternate review for governance-surface implementation, and Stage 6 closeout before issue closure.

specialist_reviews.focus: Identify the unsafe raw-hook behavior and the governance-owned manifest/build path.
specialist_reviews.summary: The raw-hook failure mode writes into product checkouts. The governance helper must resolve docs/graphify_targets.json paths relative to the governance root and must refuse unsafe output paths before build_graph.py can mkdir them.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude alternate review returned APPROVED_WITH_CHANGES. Required changes are incorporated into this plan: backlog row before implementation, architecture-scope cross-review with real PR number, governance-root manifest resolution, output preflight before build_graph.py, pinned gpt-5.3-codex-spark high implementation handoff, Stage 6 closeout, and adoption proof that raw graphify hooks are removed or absent.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: After MS1 lands, MS2 may implement only the governance-owned graphify hook helper/installer and focused tests. The worker handoff is pinned to gpt-5.3-codex-spark with model_reasoning_effort=high.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run MS2 from issue #250 with model gpt-5.3-codex-spark and model_reasoning_effort=high; do not edit AIS or knocktracker in the governance implementation branch.
blocked_on: MS1 artifacts merged or otherwise accepted.
Architecture cross-review artifact must be added with a real PR number before merge if the PR touches architecture-scope helper behavior.

material_deviation_rules: If the helper cannot determine governance root, repo slug, or manifest target deterministically, halt and update #248 before implementation continues.
If output-path safety cannot be checked before build_graph.py invocation, halt MS2 and request another alternate-family review.
If implementation requires editing AIS or knocktracker, create or update the relevant product-repo issue and stop governance-repo edits.
If existing unmanaged hook handling would silently preserve raw graphify writers, fail closed and require explicit backup or force behavior.
If a cross-review artifact is committed, it must use pr_scope: architecture and a real integer pr_number.
If Stage 6 closeout reveals additional required cleanup, complete it in scope or create an issue-backed follow-up before closing #248.
